title: ipomoea batatas var. fastigiata
intro: Ipomoea batatas var. fastigiata, commonly known as Japanese sweet potato or Japanese yam, is a variety of sweet potato with a distinctive fastigiate growth habit, characterized by a compact, upright stem and smaller tubers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. Consuming Ipomoea batatas var. fastigiata has been associated with several health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced blood sugar levels, and enhanced immune function. The tubers can be prepared for medicinal use through various methods, including boiling, steaming, or drying, and are often used in traditional herbal remedies.
